questions.prompt: For the function f(x) = 2x + 3, the graph of y = f(x) in the xy-plane is parallel to line j. What is the slope of line j?
questions.rationale: The function y = 2x + 3 is a line with slope 2. Parallel lines have equal slopes, so line j also has slope 2.

questions.prompt: One gallon of paint will cover 220 square feet of a surface. A room has a total wall area of w square feet. Which equation represents the total amount of paint P, in gallons, needed to paint the walls of the room twice?
questions.rationale: Painting twice means covering 2w square feet. Since 1 gallon covers 220 ft^2, P = (2w)/220 = w/110.

questions.prompt: The function f(x) is defined as 19 more than 4 times a number x. If y = f(x) is graphed in the xy-plane, what is the best interpretation of the x-intercept?

choices.C: The value of f(x) increases by 1 for each increase of 4 in the value of the number.
choices.D: For each increase of 1 in the value of the number, f(x) increases by 4.

questions.rationale: Here f(x) = 4x + 19. The x-intercept occurs when y = f(x) = 0, so 0 = 4x + 19 → x = −19/4. This matches choice A.

questions.prompt: The table shows the population of Greenleaf, Idaho, for the years 2000 and 2010: in 2000 the population was 862, and in 2010 the population was 846. If the relationship between population and year is linear, which function P models the population of Greenleaf t years after 2000?
Brian saves 2/5 of the $215 he earns each week from his job. If Brian continues to save at this rate, how much money, in dollars, will Brian save in 9 weeks?
h(x) = x + b. For the linear function h, b is a constant and h(0) = 45. What is the value of b?
questions.rationale: Substitute x = 0: h(0) = 0 + b = 45, so b = 45.

questions.prompt: For groups of 25 or more people, a museum charges $21 per person for the first 25 people and $14 for each additional person. Which function f gives the total charge, in dollars, for a tour group with n people, where n ≥ 25?

The boiling point of water at sea level is 212°F. For every 550 feet above sea level, the boiling point is lowered by about 1°F. Which equation can be used to find the boiling point B of water, in °F, x feet above sea level?
questions.rationale: The boiling point decreases 1°F per 550 ft, so the rate is −1/550 °F per ft. Starting at 212 gives B = 212 − x/550.

questions.prompt: w(t) = 300 − 4t models the volume of liquid, in milliliters, in a container t seconds after it begins draining. According to the model, what is the predicted volume, in milliliters, draining from the container each second?
questions.rationale: The coefficient of t is −4, meaning the volume decreases by 4 milliliters each second, so 4 mL drains per second.

questions.prompt: The equation y = 18 − 5x represents the speed y, in feet per second, of Sheila's bicycle x seconds after she applied the brakes. If the equation is graphed, which is the best interpretation of the x-coordinate of the line’s x-intercept in context?

choices.A: The speed of Sheila's bicycle, in feet per second, before Sheila applied the brakes
choices.B: The number of feet per second the speed of Sheila's bicycle decreased each second after Sheila applied the brakes
choices.C: The number of seconds it took from when Sheila began applying the brakes until the bicycle came to a complete stop
choices.D: The number of feet Sheila's bicycle traveled from when she began applying the brakes until the bicycle came to a complete stop

questions.rationale: At the x-intercept, y = 0, meaning the bicycle’s speed is 0 (it has stopped). The corresponding x-value is the time in seconds until it comes to a complete stop.
